data.content: The build confirms: the file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler never sees it because it's not added to the `Compile` item group.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and track for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file at `obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`, but it never adds that file to the `Compile` item group. The file exists on disk, but the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, after the file is written. I should also add it to `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the skill for this since it's exactly the scenario described.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation and track for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes a `.cs` file to `obj/`, but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's default glob (`**/*.cs`) evaluates at project load time — before the target runs — so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target (after writing the file) so the compiler picks it up and `dotnet clean` removes it.
